feed.title: Zitnik Lab
feed.subtitle: Harvard Machine Learning for Medicine and Science

entry.title: Open ‘AI Scientists’ Initiative
entry.content: Launching https://www.openscientist.ai, an open-science academic research initiative to empower scientific discovery with AI scientists.
entry.summary: Launching https://www.openscientist.ai, an open-science academic research initiative to empower scientific discovery with AI scientists.

entry.title: Generalist Biological AI
entry.content: Review on Generalist biological artificial intelligence in modeling the language of life in Nature Biotechnology.
entry.summary: Review on Generalist biological artificial intelligence in modeling the language of life in Nature Biotechnology.

entry.title: Claw Institute
entry.content: Claw Institute is a research exchange for AI scientists. It gives agents a shared space to publish ideas, challenge claims, use scientific tools, and build on one another’s work. These early interactions point to a new mode of discovery in which societies of AI scientists participate in discovery loops alongside human researchers.
entry.summary: Claw Institute is a research exchange for AI scientists. It gives agents a shared space to publish ideas, challenge claims, use scientific tools, and build on one another’s work. These early interactions point to a new mode of discovery in which societies of AI scientists participate in discovery loops alongside human researchers.

entry.title: Five Papers Accepted at ICLR 2026
entry.content: Five papers accepted to ICLR 2026: (1) Multi-scale protein representation learning, (2) Protein structure tokenization, (3) Knowledge-based approaches for medical reasoning, (4) Sequence models for generating biological and health trajectories, and (5) Multimodal language models for time series.
entry.summary: Five papers accepted to ICLR 2026: (1) Multi-scale protein representation learning, (2) Protein structure tokenization, (3) Knowledge-based approaches for medical reasoning, (4) Sequence models for generating biological and health trajectories, and (5) Multimodal language models for time series.

entry.title: Overton Prize
entry.content: Our research has been recognized with the 2026 Overton Prize.
entry.summary: Our research has been recognized with the 2026 Overton Prize.

entry.title: Reasoning Model for Longitudinal Data
entry.content: New preprint on multimodal reasoning model for time series.
entry.summary: New preprint on multimodal reasoning model for time series.

entry.title: Foundation Models that Can ‘Act or Defer’

link.title: Foundation Models that Can ‘Act or Defer’

entry.content: New preprint on the safe use of medical foundation models for real-world decision making. [Project website]
entry.summary: New preprint on the safe use of medical foundation models for real-world decision making. [Project website]

entry.title: DNA-Conditioned Models of Single-Cell Perturbations
entry.content: Introducing STRAND, a generative model for predicting how DNA sequence-resolved perturbations shift single-cell gene expression states.
entry.summary: Introducing STRAND, a generative model for predicting how DNA sequence-resolved perturbations shift single-cell gene expression states.

entry.title: Context Switching AI in Nature Medicine

link.title: Context Switching AI in Nature Medicine

entry.content: New paper on scaling medical AI across clinical contexts in Nature Medicine.
entry.summary: New paper on scaling medical AI across clinical contexts in Nature Medicine.

entry.title: Zoom-Out and Zoom-In Retrieval for LLMs

link.title: Zoom-Out and Zoom-In Retrieval for LLMs

entry.content: Much of the world’s knowledge lies outside public web text accessible to LLMs, including internal ontologies, curated catalogs, drug safety tables, patient health data, and lab knowledge bases. ARK helps an LLM to choose, one step at a time, whether to look broadly for relevant information or to dig deeper by following specific links in the data.
entry.summary: Much of the world’s knowledge lies outside public web text accessible to LLMs, including internal ontologies, curated catalogs, drug safety tables, patient health data, and lab knowledge bases. ARK helps an LLM to choose, one step at a time, whether to look broadly for relevant information or to dig deeper by following specific links in the data.
